qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public MongoDB explain queries expose database internals without authentication
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about allowPublicExplain defaulting to true, exposing MongoDB execution plans. The advisory describes session token exfiltration via the redirectClassNameForKey query parameter due to missing authorization checks after query redirect. Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different impact.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes session token exfiltration via the redirectClassNameForKey query parameter in parse-server (CWE-863: Incorrect Authorization). The sole qualifying finding (public_explain_info_disclosure) targets a different vulnerability: allowPublicExplain defaulting to true, exposing MongoDB execution plans. It does not involve redirectClassNameForKey, session token access, or the missing authorization-check-after-query-redirect root cause.
evidence: Advisory describes redirectClassNameForKey query parameter allowing session token exfiltration due to missing security checks after query redirect.
public_explain_info_disclosure targets allowPublicExplain default in src/rest.js and src/Options/Definitions.js, unrelated to redirectClassNameForKey.
